key_insight: The paper enables end-to-end learning of high-dimensional feedback controllers while preserving hard safety constraints by embedding a control-barrier-function safety filter as a differentiable optimization layer.
engineering_relevance: This makes scalable safe control more practical for large multi-agent systems, where learning-based policies must satisfy strict real-time safety requirements.
method: Use a quadratic-program-based CBF safety filter inside the policy network.
Train with operator splitting to handle the embedded optimization efficiently.
Apply Jacobian-Free Backpropagation (JFB) to avoid costly differentiation through the solver.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful tests on high-dimensional multi-agent nonlinear control problems with state dimensions up to 1200 and control dimensions up to 400.
watch_item: The approach still relies on solving an optimization layer during control, so real-time performance and solver robustness may depend on problem conditioning and implementation details.
paper_title: End-to-End Learning of Safe Optimal Feedback Control in High Dimensions with Control Barrier Function Layers
